2604.14773.title: CoPA: Benchmarking Personalized Question Answering with Data-Informed Cognitive Factors
2604.14773.paper_summary: While LLMs have demonstrated remarkable potential in Question Answering (QA), evaluating personalization remains a critical bottleneck. Existing paradigms predominantly rely on lexical-level similarity or manual heuristics, often lacking sufficient data-driven validation. We address this by mining Community-Individual Preference Divergence (CIPD), where individual choices override consensus, to distill six key personalization factors as evaluative dimensions. Accordingly, we introduce CoPA, a benchmark with 1,985 user profiles for fine-grained, factor-level assessment. By quantifying the alignment between model outputs and user-specific cognitive preferences inferred from interaction patterns, CoPA provides a more comprehensive and discriminative standard for evaluating personalized QA than generic metrics. The code is available at https://github.com/bjzgcai/CoPA.
2604.14773.paper_summary_zh: 雖然大型語言模型在問題回答（QA）方面展現了顯著的潛力，但評估個性化仍然是一個關鍵瓶頸。現有的範式主要依賴於詞彙層級的相似性或手動啟發式，往往缺乏足夠的數據驅動驗證。我們通過挖掘社群-個人偏好差異（CIPD）來解決這個問題，在這裡，個體選擇超越共識，提煉出六個關鍵的個性化因素作為評估維度。因此，我們推出了 CoPA，一個擁有 1,985 個用戶檔案的基準，用於細緻的因素級評估。通過量化模型輸出與從互動模式推斷的用戶特定認知偏好的對齊程度，CoPA 提供了一個比通用指標更全面和具區分性的個性化 QA 評估標準。代碼可在 https://github.com/bjzgcai/CoPA 獲得。
2604.14773.author: Hang Su et.al.
2604.14773.authors: Hang Su, Zequn Liu, Chen Hu, Xuesong Lu, Yingce Xia, Zhen Liu
